tluaGenericPackTailCall.tlua(27,8): error TLUA2769: No overload matches this call.
  The last overload gave the following error.
    Argument of type 'boolean' is not assignable to parameter of type 'string'.


==== tluaGenericPackTailCall.tlua (1 errors) ====
    declare function two(): (number, string);
    declare function sinkNumber(...: any): number;
    declare function sink3(a: boolean, b: number, c: string): void;
    
    // A generic, overloaded tail call expands its resolved result pack.
    local r = sinkNumber(pcall(two));
    sink3(pcall(two));
    
    // A non-generic tail call keeps the existing expansion path.
    declare function sink2(a: number, b: string): void;
    sink2(two());
    
    // A generic scalar return still declines tail expansion, allowing the outer
    // parameter type to contextually type the function passed through id.
    declare function id<T>(x: T): T;
    declare function outer(callback: (value: number) => number): void;
    outer(id(function(value) return value; end));
    
    // Passing overloaded pcall as a value is separate from tail-pack expansion.
    local nestedOk, nestedInnerOk, nestedValue = pcall(pcall, two);
    
    // A failed overload resolution must not treat its synthetic intersection
    // return as a pack.
    declare function f(x: number): (number, string);
    declare function f(x: string): (boolean, number);
    declare function sink(...: any): void;
    sink(f(true));
           ~~~~
!!! error TLUA2769: No overload matches this call.
!!! error TLUA2769:   The last overload gave the following error.
!!! error TLUA2769:     Argument of type 'boolean' is not assignable to parameter of type 'string'.
!!! related TLUA2771 tluaGenericPackTailCall.tlua:25:18: The last overload is declared here.
